steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content:
A line with slope $-\frac{1}{4}$ has the form
$$
y=-\frac{1}{4}x+b
$$

steps.title: Find the intercept
steps.content:
Substitute the point $(8{,}5)$ and solve for the missing intercept:
$$
5=-\frac{1}{4}(8)+b
$$
$$
5=-2+b
$$
$$
b=7
$$

steps.title: Write the line equation
steps.content: The equation is $$ y=-\frac{x}{4}+7 $$ The answer is D.
